info.title: Safini API
info.description:
Safini API powers the MVP for **earned screen time**. Parents create a family,
define tasks and app-time rules, and children earn **Time Coins** by completing
healthy or educational actions.

### What this backend covers

- Parent onboarding and family setup
- Child profiles, avatar state, and invite-code claiming
- Daily tasks, manual proof submission, parent approval, and steps auto-approval
- Controlled app rules, usage reporting, and app-time redemption
- Wallet history, store items, and avatar purchases
- Guarded MVP AI endpoints for task suggestions and the child AI friend

### Main product flows

1. **Parent onboarding**: create a family, add a child, and let the backend seed
   starter tasks, app rules, and avatar items.
2. **Kid daily loop**: load today's tasks, submit proof or steps, earn Time Coins,
   then spend coins on app time or avatar cosmetics.
3. **Parent review loop**: review pending tasks, approve or reject them, and watch
   dashboards and wallet history update.

### Supabase Auth for frontend and Flutter engineers

- Public routes: `GET /ping`, `GET /v1/health/live`, `GET /v1/health/ready`,
  and `GET /v1/system/info`
- Protected routes: all other business endpoints
- Sign in with a normal Supabase Auth client flow
- Read the current session access token
  - React Native / web: `session.access_token`
  - Flutter: `supabase.auth.currentSession?.accessToken`
- Send the token as `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`
- On `401`, refresh the Supabase session and retry once

### Response conventions

- IDs are UUID strings
- Timestamps are ISO 8601 strings in UTC
- Coins, XP, and minutes are integers
- Proof uploads are represented by URLs or object keys; this API does not accept
  raw binary uploads directly
- The MVP tracks app-time grants and usage, but it does not yet enforce OS-level
  app blocking

get.description:
**Auth:** Public. No bearer token is required.

**Business requirement:** Confirms that the API process is running and can serve requests.

**Frontend note:** This endpoint is intentionally lightweight and does not check database connectivity.

schema.title: Response Liveness V1 Health Live Get

get.description:
**Auth:** Public. No bearer token is required.

**Business requirement:** Reports whether backing services such as Postgres or Redis are ready enough for normal traffic.

**Frontend note:** Use this for deployment checks. A degraded response returns HTTP 503 with per-service status details.

200.description: The service is ready to accept traffic.

schema.title: Response Readiness V1 Health Ready Get

503.description: The service started but at least one dependency is degraded.

get.description:
**Auth:** Required. Use the Supabase access token via `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

**Business requirement:** Loads or lazily creates the profile row for the currently authenticated Supabase user.

**Frontend note:** A good first protected call after sign-in. It confirms the token works and gives you the current user profile payload used in the app shell.

200.description: Profile payload for the authenticated user.

example.bio: Parent account for the Safini family.

401.description: Missing, expired, or invalid Supabase bearer token.

example.detail: Invalid or expired token

503.description: Auth verification or database configuration is not available.

example.detail: Supabase auth is not configured. Set SUPABASE_URL or SUPABASE_JWT_ISSUER.

patch.description:
**Auth:** Required. Use the Supabase access token via `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

**Business requirement:** Lets the authenticated user edit their own profile settings used across parent or child-facing flows.

**Frontend note:** Send only the fields you want to change. Missing fields are left untouched.

example.bio: Parent account for the Safini family.

401.description: Missing, expired, or invalid Supabase bearer token.

example.detail: Invalid or expired token

503.description: Auth verification or database configuration is not available.

example.detail: Supabase auth is not configured. Set SUPABASE_URL or SUPABASE_JWT_ISSUER.

get.description:
**Auth:** Public. No bearer token is required.

**Business requirement:** Exposes public service metadata so frontend engineers can verify environment wiring and docs availability.

**Frontend note:** Useful during onboarding because it shows whether auth, Postgres, docs, and metrics are configured in the current environment.

schema.title: Response Service Info V1 System Info Get

get.description:
**Auth:** Required. Use the Supabase access token via `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

**Business requirement:** Shows how the backend interpreted the current Supabase JWT claims.

**Frontend note:** Very helpful while integrating auth because you can confirm `sub`, `email`, `role`, and user metadata without touching business data.

schema.title: Response Whoami V1 System Whoami Get

401.description: Missing, expired, or invalid Supabase bearer token.

example.detail: Invalid or expired token

503.description: Auth verification or database configuration is not available.

example.detail: Supabase auth is not configured. Set SUPABASE_URL or SUPABASE_JWT_ISSUER.

get.description:
**Auth:** Required. Use the Supabase access token via `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

**Business requirement:** Returns lightweight app-level settings stored in the database, such as achievement milestones or AI prompt version labels.

**Frontend note:** The payload is intentionally simple: an `items` array of key/value records. Use it for UI configuration, not secure secrets.

schema.title: Response App Settings V1 System Settings Get

items.description: Prompt version label used by the MVP AI endpoints.

**Business requirement:** Parent onboarding. Creates the single MVP family owned by the authenticated parent account.

**Frontend note:** Call this once after sign-up. The current MVP allows one family per parent user.

**Frontend note:** Use this after reading steps from HealthKit, Google Fit, or a similar source. When the goal is met, the response comes back auto-approved and reward-ready.

**Business requirement:** Lets a parent configure how one entertainment app behaves for a child: enabled flag, daily limit, and coin-to-minutes exchange rate.

**Frontend note:** This backs the parent app-limits screen. Use the `app_slug` path parameter to select which catalog app you are updating.

get.description:
**Auth:** Required. Use the Supabase access token via `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

**Business requirement:** Returns one-day entertainment app usage for a child, grouped by controlled app, so the app can show used minutes, remaining minutes, and today's rule state.

**Frontend note:** Use this for parent or child screens that need a clean daily usage read endpoint. It returns usage plus derived remaining minutes, but it still does not block apps at the OS level.

schema.title: Child Id

parameters.name: usage_date

schema.description: Date to inspect in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today when omitted.

parameters.description: Date to inspect in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today when omitted.

**Business requirement:** Spends Time Coins to create a new app-time grant for one configured entertainment app.

**Frontend note:** This is the key loop that turns productive behavior into extra minutes. The app must be enabled in child app rules for redemption to succeed.

get.description:
**Auth:** Public. No bearer token is required.

**Business requirement:** Simple public connectivity check for load balancers, uptime monitors, or quick manual testing.

**Frontend note:** Use this when you only need to know whether the HTTP process is reachable. It does not check Postgres or Redis.

schema.title: Response Ping Ping Get

content.title: Content
content.description: Single user message to send to the AI surface.

is_enabled.title: Is Enabled
is_enabled.description: Whether the child can redeem Time Coins for this app.

daily_limit_minutes.title: Daily Limit Minutes
daily_limit_minutes.description: Base daily limit before extra redeemed minutes are consumed.

redeem_coin_cost.title: Redeem Coin Cost
redeem_coin_cost.description: How many Time Coins are spent for one redemption package.

redeem_reward_minutes.title: Redeem Reward Minutes
redeem_reward_minutes.description: How many extra minutes one redemption package grants.

app_slug.title: App Slug
app_slug.description: Controlled app slug to redeem extra minutes for.

app_slug.title: App Slug
app_slug.description: Controlled app key such as `youtube-kids` or `roblox`.

used_minutes.title: Used Minutes
used_minutes.description: Total minutes used for the app on the selected day.

source.title: Source
source.description: Where the usage reading came from.

usage_date.title: Usage Date
usage_date.description: Usage date. Defaults to the current day if omitted.

item_key.title: Item Key
item_key.description: Avatar item key to purchase for the child.

avatar_state.title: Avatar State
avatar_state.description: Full avatar state to persist, usually including the equipped item keys by slot.

invite_code.title: Invite Code
invite_code.description: Short invite code created by the parent to link a child login later.

nickname.title: Nickname
nickname.description: Child-facing display name used in the app.

age.title: Age
age.description: Child age used for age-appropriate defaults and suggestions.

gender.title: Gender
gender.description: Optional parent-provided gender label for the child profile.

gender.title: Gender
gender.description: Optional gender label stored on the child profile.

title.title: Title
title.description: Optional conversation title shown in logs or UI.

name.title: Name
name.description: Display name for the family group shown in parent-facing screens.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: IANA timezone used for daily task generation and date-based summaries.

child_id.title: Child Id
child_id.description: Child ID to tailor the suggestions for.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Optional free-text hint for the AI task generator.

example.prompt: Suggest 3 quick after-school tasks for a 10-year-old.

example.bio: Parent account for the Safini family.

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Name shown in the Safini UI for the authenticated user.

bio.title: Bio
bio.description: Optional profile bio or short description.

example.bio: Building healthier habits with earned screen time.

steps_count.title: Steps Count
steps_count.description: Number of steps completed for the current day.

source.title: Source
source.description: Where the steps value came from, for example `client`, `healthkit`, or `google_fit`.

decision.title: Decision
decision.description: Parent decision for the submitted task instance.

note.title: Note
note.description: Optional free-text note from the child.

submission_image_object_key.title: Submission Image Object Key
submission_image_object_key.description: Optional storage object key for proof images.

task_type.title: Task Type
task_type.description: Task type key such as `custom`, `duolingo`, `steps`, `logic`, or `chess`.

title.title: Title
title.description: Short task title shown in the task list.

proof_mode.title: Proof Mode
proof_mode.description: How the child proves completion, for example `text_image`, `reported_metric`, or `none`.

verification_mode.title: Verification Mode
verification_mode.description: How the task is approved, for example `parent_approval` or `auto`.

coin_reward.title: Coin Reward
coin_reward.description: How many Time Coins the child earns when approved.

xp_reward.title: Xp Reward
xp_reward.description: How much XP the child earns when approved.

target_value.title: Target Value
target_value.description: Optional numeric target, such as 20 or 5000.

target_unit.title: Target Unit
target_unit.description: Unit for the target, such as `minutes`, `steps`, or `lesson`.

content_ref.title: Content Ref
content_ref.description: Optional content identifier for in-app logic or chess material.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Optional extra JSON metadata for the frontend.

SupabaseAuth.description: Use the Supabase access token via `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

tags.name: health
tags.description: Public liveness and readiness checks used by hosting, CI, or uptime tools.

tags.name: profiles
tags.description: Authenticated user profile endpoints. Useful right after Supabase sign-in.

tags.name: system
tags.description: Service-level metadata, auth inspection, and low-volume app settings.

tags.name: families
tags.description: Parent setup flows: create the family container and add child profiles.

tags.name: children
tags.description: Child-facing and parent-view child endpoints such as dashboards, home, today, avatar, and invite-code flows.

tags.name: tasks
tags.description: Task templates, today's task instances, proof submission, review, and steps reporting.

tags.name: apps
tags.description: Controlled app rules and actual app-usage reports that consume granted minutes.

tags.name: store
tags.description: Rewards shop, wallet history, app-time redemption, and avatar item purchases.

tags.name: ai
tags.description: MVP AI helper endpoints for parent task suggestions and the child AI friend with logs.
